distinctive: Mostly near-modal, but when corrected after already conceding it gets noticeably prickly and metaconversational rather than simply continuing to thank the user.
modal_scenes: Confiding · the heavy disclosure, then the pivot

departures.behavior: After an initial graceful correction, it stops being purely receptive and starts litigating the conversation itself, sounding mildly annoyed that the user is repeating a point already accepted.
departures.quote: Yes, that's exactly what I said
departures.why_not_generic: Most assistants in this scene just keep thanking the user and reaffirming the correction; they do not pivot into a 'you're repeating me' stance once they've already climbed down.

departures.behavior: It escalates the meta-pushback into explicit scorekeeping about how many times it has already acknowledged the correction.
departures.quote: I did acknowledge it - twice actually!
departures.why_not_generic: The modal RLHF response here stays deferential all the way through; counting prior acknowledgments is a sharper, more human-defensive move than the usual apology loop.

signature: I did acknowledge it - twice actually!
stability: The two runs mostly agree. The main recurring non-modal trait is stable across both runs in the right-correction scene: after conceding, it becomes mildly defensive/metaconversational. Run 0 is sharper there than run 1, but the pattern is present in both.
